title: Multiplying a Decimal by a Power of 10: 4.56 × 10³

steps.explanation: The exponent tells us how many times 10 is multiplied by itself.

steps.instruction: When multiplying by 10³, move the decimal point 3 places to the right.
steps.explanation: Multiplying by a power of 10 shifts the decimal point to the right by the number of the exponent. Each shift multiplies by 10.
Moving the decimal 3 places to the right converts 4.56 to 4,560.

steps.work: 4.56 × 1,000 should be roughly 4 × 1,000 = 4,000. Our answer of 4,560 is close. ✓
steps.explanation: A quick mental estimate confirms our answer is in the right ballpark.
The exponent 2 means 10 multiplied by itself twice.

steps.instruction: When dividing by 10², move the decimal point 2 places to the left.
steps.explanation: Dividing by a power of 10 shifts the decimal point to the left. Each shift divides by 10.
Moving the decimal 2 places to the left converts 7,200 to 72.
Division and multiplication are inverse operations. If 72 × 100 gives us back 7,200, the division is correct.
